mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Mercedes-Benz CLA180/200 petrol engine costs £2,500–£4,500 supply only, varying by generation: M270 £2,500–£4,500, M282 £3,000–£5,500. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 petrol reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: That depends on which generation. The C117 M270 (2013–2019) has known timing chain stretch and ignition system issues. The C118 M282 (2019+) is a more modern engine with better reliability, cylinder shutoff technology, and fewer common faults. Buy the C118 if you can.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: C117 M270: timing chain stretch (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500), ignition system faults (misfires, £200–£600), carbon build-up (walnut blasting, £400–£800), thermostat housing leaks (£300–£600). C118 M282: carbon build-up, cylinder shutoff issues (hesitation, £500–£1,500).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: CLA values range from £5,000–£25,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £2,500–£4,500 reconditioned engine represents 10–80% of vehicle value — our 70% Mercedes-Benz scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained C118, replacement is often justified. For an ageing C117 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CLA180/200 has used two engines: M270 (C117, 2013–2019, 1.6L turbo, 122–156 bhp) and M282 (C118, 2019+, 1.3L turbo, 136–163 bhp). Check your VIN or registration year. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M270 timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£2,500–£4,500). Replacing the chain, guides and tensioner is recommended around 60,000–100,000 miles. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic engine failure.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges across both CLA petrol generations. Reconditioned units are available from specialist Mercedes engine rebuilders. Prices for M282 typically command a premium over M270 due to newer technology. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLA180/200 petrol engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent Mercedes specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The M282's cylinder shutoff and direct injection add complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * C118 (2019+) with M282 — the more modern and reliable engine
* C117 (2013–2019) with documented timing chain history (M270)
* Full service history with regular oil changes and MB-approved oil
* M270 with evidence of carbon clean (walnut blasting) performed
avoidIf: * C117 with any cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch risk
* C117 with misfires or rough idle without documented diagnosis
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect
* M282 with cylinder shutoff hesitation without documented software update

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLA180/200 petrol is a stylish, compact four-door coupé with a choice of two modern turbo petrol engines. The C117 M270 (2013–2019) is a decent engine with known timing chain and ignition issues; the C118 M282 (2019+) is a significant improvement with better reliability and cylinder shutoff technology. Buy the C118 if you can, or a well-maintained C117 with documented timing work. Maintain the oil and ignition systems, and the CLA will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare Mercedes-Benz CLA180/200 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz CLA180 / CLA200 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup — M270 timing chain stretch warning
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — pre-2019 = M270, 2019+ = M282
* Timing Chain History — M270: chains replaced? Tensioner checked? (critical)
* Ignition System — Any misfires, rough idle, or starting issues? (M270 common)
* Carbon Build-up — M270/M282: has intake had a walnut blast? (every 60k miles recommended)
* Cooling System — Any temperature fluctuations? (M270 thermostat housing leaks)
* Cylinder Shutoff (M282) — Any hesitation or check engine light? (software updates available)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
